source_file: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP within 180 days of graduation, not the day your study permit expires
heuristics.condition: When you have graduated from an eligible Canadian institution and plan to stay and work in Canada...
heuristics.action: Submit PGWP application as early as possible after receiving official proof of graduation, but no later than 180 days post-graduation
heuristics.because: The source explicitly states the 180-day window as a hard deadline after which eligibility is lost; the poster applied on day 3 post-graduation

heuristics.rule: Apply online (网申) rather than by paper for faster processing
heuristics.condition: When submitting a PGWP application in the current IRCC environment...
heuristics.action: Use the online portal (IMM 5710 online) rather than paper application
heuristics.because: The poster explicitly chose online-only and achieved a 5-week turnaround; a community commenter signals awareness that IRCC may have moved fully to online applications

heuristics.rule: If you have previously submitted biometrics, expect a faster PGWP processing timeline due to biometric exemption
heuristics.condition: When you have already provided biometrics during a prior Canadian visa/permit process...
heuristics.action: Note your biometric exemption status in your application; do not plan extra time for biometric appointment delays
heuristics.because: The poster's 5-week timeline was partly enabled by biometric exemption at day 15 rather than requiring a fresh appointment

decision_factors.why_it_matters: The length of your Canadian study program directly determines the maximum duration of PGWP you can receive (up to 3 years for programs of 2+ years)
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your official program duration as listed on your study permit and transcripts; confirm with registrar if your program was non-standard length (e.g., 1.5 years)

decision_factors.why_it_matters: Having previously submitted biometrics removes a processing step that can add weeks to PGWP timelines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your IRCC account or prior permit documents to confirm whether biometrics are on file and still valid

decision_factors.factor: Completeness and quality of graduation documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: IRCC requires a graduation confirmation letter from the registrar specifying degree name and completion date — missing or vague documentation causes delays or refusals
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the letter directly from the registrar's office and confirm it explicitly states degree name and official completion date; do not substitute with an unofficial transcript or degree certificate alone

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Remaining in Canada under implied status while PGWP is processing
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: Implied status does NOT permit full-time work. Students often assume that because they can legally remain, they can also work — but they cannot work beyond what their student permit explicitly allowed until the PGWP is approved
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Graduates who receive job offers and want to start immediately after graduation, or those who assume their on-campus/part-time work authorization continues automatically

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Leaving Canada while PGWP application is in progress (implied status)
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: Departing Canada while under implied status may terminate that status entirely, leaving the applicant unable to re-enter without a valid visa/permit. This is a high-stakes risk that the community is actively asking about but the post does not answer
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Graduates who want to visit family abroad between graduation and PGWP approval, or those who travel for job interviews

failure_patterns.pattern: Waiting too long after graduation to gather documents and submit PGWP application
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Graduates assume the 180-day window is generous and delay while waiting for official transcripts, degree certificates, or registrar letters — which themselves can take weeks to obtain
failure_patterns.consequence: Missing the 180-day eligibility window entirely, permanently losing PGWP eligibility for that degree
warning_signs: Student asks 'can I apply after my study permit expires?' rather than 'how fast can I apply after graduation?'
Student conflates the study permit expiry date with the PGWP application deadline

failure_patterns.pattern: Working full-time during implied status before PGWP is approved
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Graduates misread 'implied status allows you to stay' as 'implied status allows you to continue all prior activities including work'
failure_patterns.consequence: Unauthorized work in Canada, which can jeopardize the PGWP application and future immigration applications
warning_signs: Graduate says 'I can work because I'm on implied status'
Graduate starts a full-time job immediately after graduation before receiving PGWP approval letter

diagnostic_questions.question: How long was your Canadian study program — specifically, was it under 1 year, exactly 1 year, between 1 and 2 years, or 2 years or more?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: This determines the maximum PGWP duration you are eligible for, which affects your entire post-graduation immigration strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: you qualify for a 3-year PGWP — plan your Express Entry or PNP timeline accordingly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 2 years: PGWP duration is capped at program length or 1 year — assess whether this is sufficient for your immigration pathway or whether bridging options are needed

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already submitted biometrics to IRCC during a prior permit or visa application?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether a biometric appointment will add processing time to your PGWP application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm biometrics are still valid (10-year validity); expect faster processing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Budget additional time and cost for biometric enrollment; locate the nearest collection site in advance

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your registrar's office issued a formal graduation confirmation letter that explicitly states your degree name and completion date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether your documentation is IRCC-compliant; unofficial transcripts or degree certificates alone are insufficient
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with application; verify the letter uses the exact degree name as on your study permit
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Request this letter immediately — processing time at registrar's office can consume weeks of your 180-day window

industry_realities.common_belief: Once your study permit expires, you must leave Canada or your status is illegal
industry_realities.actual_reality: If you have submitted a PGWP application before your study permit expired, you are in 'implied status' and may legally remain in Canada while the application is processed
industry_realities.implication: Graduates do not need to leave Canada between graduation and PGWP approval, but they must have submitted the application before the study permit expires to benefit from implied status

industry_realities.common_belief: PGWP duration is simply '3 years for any graduate student'
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP duration is tied to the length of your study program, capped at 3 years. A 1-year program yields approximately 1 year of PGWP; a 1.5-year program does not automatically round up; a 2+ year program yields 3 years
industry_realities.implication: Students in accelerated or non-standard programs must calculate their expected PGWP duration carefully before making post-graduation immigration plans

junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Assumes implied status = full legal authorization to work and travel as normal; treats it as equivalent to having a valid permit
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Understands implied status preserves only the right to remain in Canada; does not extend work authorization beyond what the expiring study permit allowed; knows that leaving Canada likely terminates implied status
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Acting on the naive assumption can result in unauthorized work or being stranded outside Canada without valid entry documents

junior_vs_senior_signals.dimension: PGWP duration expectations for non-standard program lengths
junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Asks 'will my 1.5-year program give me 1 or 1.5 years of PGWP — does it round up?' — treats this as an obvious question with an obvious answer
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Looks up the specific IRCC policy on program length calculation; understands that PGWP duration mirrors program length without automatic rounding, and plans immigration pathway around the actual likely duration
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: A 6-month difference in PGWP duration can determine whether you have enough time to accumulate Canadian work experience for Express Entry or a PNP

community_misconceptions.misconception: Implied status during PGWP processing allows you to work full-time in Canada
community_misconceptions.why_believed: The phrase 'implied status' sounds like full legal status continuation; students hear 'you can stay' and assume all prior permissions including work authorization carry over
community_misconceptions.actual_rule: Implied status only preserves the right to remain physically in Canada. Work authorization during this period is limited to what the expiring study permit explicitly permitted (e.g., on-campus work or co-op if applicable). Full-time off-campus work is not permitted until the PGWP is approved.
community_misconceptions.real_consequence: Graduates who start full-time jobs immediately after graduation under implied status are working without authorization, which can result in PGWP refusal and negative marks on future immigration applications

community_misconceptions.misconception: The PGWP application deadline is tied to when your study permit expires, not when you graduate
community_misconceptions.why_believed: Students track permit expiry dates closely throughout their studies and conflate permit expiry with the PGWP eligibility window
community_misconceptions.actual_rule: The 180-day PGWP application window begins from the date of graduation (program completion), not the study permit expiry date. These dates can differ significantly.
community_misconceptions.real_consequence: Students who wait thinking they have until their permit expires may miss the 180-day graduation-anchored window if their permit expires well after graduation

real_case_outcomes.situation: Canadian 2-year master's graduate applied for PGWP 3 days after graduation, submitted all required documents online including registrar letter, had prior biometrics on file
real_case_outcomes.decision: Submitted online application on day 3 post-graduation with complete documentation; paid CAD $255 fee
real_case_outcomes.outcome: AOR received day 2; biometric exemption confirmed day 15; approval letter and eTA issued day 35; physical work permit received day 40. Total: ~5 weeks from application submission to permit in hand
real_case_outcomes.lesson: Applying immediately after graduation with complete documentation and prior biometrics on file can result in PGWP processing as fast as 5 weeks. The bottleneck is documentation readiness, not IRCC processing speed alone.

psychological_barriers.trigger: Graduate asks whether they can leave Canada during implied status or whether their work authorization continues automatically
psychological_barriers.underlying_state: Ambiguity aversion and status anxiety — the graduate is in a liminal legal state (no valid permit, not yet approved) and is seeking reassurance that normal life can continue uninterrupted
psychological_barriers.how_it_manifests: Posts multiple clarifying questions about the edges of implied status rather than accepting the uncertainty and staying put; may be tempted to travel home or start work prematurely to feel 'normal'
psychological_barriers.expert_move: Reframe the implied status period as a short, defined waiting window (typically weeks to a couple of months), not an indefinite limbo. Advise: stay in Canada, do not work off-campus, use the time to prepare for job search. The risk of acting during this window vastly outweighs the discomfort of waiting.

psychological_barriers.trigger: Student with a 1 or 1.5-year program asks whether PGWP will 'round up' or if there's any way to extend it
psychological_barriers.underlying_state: Sunk cost and outcome anxiety — the student has invested significantly in their Canadian education and fears that a short PGWP will not be enough time to achieve permanent residency
psychological_barriers.how_it_manifests: Seeks loopholes or workarounds ('is there any way to extend?') rather than planning realistically around the actual PGWP duration they will receive
psychological_barriers.expert_move: Acknowledge the constraint directly, then redirect to concrete pathway planning: 'A 1-year PGWP is tight for Express Entry but not impossible — here is what you need to do in year one to maximize your CRS score and PNP options.' Replace hope for a rule exception with an actionable plan.

reframing_moves.original_framing: Implied status means I can stay in Canada, so I should be fine to keep working and living normally while I wait for my PGWP
reframing_moves.reframe: Implied status preserves only your right to be physically present — it does not preserve work authorization. These are two separate legal permissions.
reframing_moves.why_it_works: It separates the concept of 'right to remain' from 'right to work,' which students conflate because their study permit bundled both. Splitting them makes the restriction concrete and actionable.
reframing_moves.example_language: 你可以待在加拿大，但不代表你可以工作——这是两件不同的事
